channel.title: Numbers on Christian Internet Resources
channel.description: Recent content in Numbers on Christian Internet Resources

item.title: Numbers 1
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 1

Census of Israel’s Troops

1 On the first day, second month, second year after they left Egypt, the LORD told Moses at Sinai in the Tent of Meeting, 

2 “Take a census by families. Count the men 3 20 years old and up that can go to war. You and Aaron count them by armies. 4 To help you, appoint one man from each tribe as head of his ancestor’s house. 5 They’ll stand with you:

item.title: Numbers 2
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 2

The Layout of Israel’s Camp

2:[1] 2“The Israelites will camp with the banners of their families facing the Tent of Meeting. 3-4 On the east side of the Tabernacle, Judah will camp by its standard—the army of Nahshon (74,600). 5-6 Issachar will camp next to him—the army of Nethanel (54,400). 7-8 Zebulun will camp with its leader Eliab (57,400). 9 The Judah camp totals 186,400. It will move out first.

item.title: Numbers 3
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 3

1 The Generations of Aaron and Moses

When the LORD Spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai

Duties of Levites

2 The sons of Aaron were Nadab, the firstborn, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar— 3 anointed and ordained as priests. 4 In the Wilderness of Sinai, Nadab and Abihu died from offering before the LORD, fire different from what he’d commanded. They didn’t have any children, so Eleazar and Ithamar served as priests during Aaron’s lifetime.

item.title: Numbers 4
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 4

Duties of the Kohathites

1 The LORD told Moses and Aaron,

2 “Count the descendants of Kohath by families 3 30-50 years old who serve in the Tent of Meeting. 4 This is the work of Kohath’s descendants with the most holy objects. 5 When the camp sets out, Aaron and his sons will go in and take down the screen and cover the Ark of Testimony with it. 6 They’ll lay a dolphin-skin covering over it, spread a pure blue cloth over it, and insert its poles. 7 Over the table for the Bread of the Presence, they’ll spread a pure blue cloth and put on it the dishes, spoons, sacrificial bowls, liquid offering jars, and the continual bread that’s on it. 8 They’ll spread a scarlet cloth over them, cover it with a dolphin-skin covering, and insert its poles. 9 Then they’ll take a blue cloth and cover the lampstand, its lamps, snuffers, trays, and oil jars. 10 They’ll put it and its utensils in a dolphin-skin covering and put it on the carrying bars. 11 They’ll spread a blue cloth over the gold altar, and cover it with a dolphin-skin covering, and insert its bars. 12 They’ll take the utensils for service in the sanctuary, put them in a blue cloth, cover them with a dolphin-skin covering, and put them on the carrying bars. 13 Then they’ll take away the ashes from the altar and spread a purple cloth over it. 14 They’ll put its utensils on it: the firepans, forks, shovels, and basins. They’ll spread a dolphin-skin covering over it and insert its poles.

item.title: Numbers 5
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 5

Purity in Israel’s Camp

1 The LORD told Moses,

         2“Tell Israel to send outside the camp any leper, anybody with a discharge, anyone ceremonially unclean because of a dead person. 3 Send out male or female so they won’t defile their camp where I live.”

4 They did that.

item.title: Numbers 6
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 6

The Nazirite Vow

1-2 The LORD told Moses to say to Israel,

         “When men or women take a Nazarite vow to dedicate themselves to the LORD, 3 they must abstain from wine, alcohol, and from vinegar made from them. They can’t drink grape juice or eat fresh grapes or raisins. 4 They’re not to eat anything a grapevine produces from seeds to skin.

item.title: Numbers 7
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 7

Offerings of Dedication

7:1The day Moses finished setting up the Tabernacle, he anointed and consecrated its furnishings, the altar, and its utensils. 2 The leaders in Israel (besides the numbered men) made an offering 3 and brought it to the Tabernacle: 6 covered carts and 12 cattle, a cart for each pair of leaders and an ox for each leader. 4 The LORD told Moses, 5“Accept these things for use in the Tent of Meeting. Give them to each Levite according to his service.” 6 Moses accepted the carts and cattle and gave them to the Levites: 7 2 carts and 4 cattle to Gershon’s descendants to match their service, 8 and 4 carts and 8 cattle to Merari’s descendants to match their service—as directed by Ithamar son of Aaron. 9 He didn’t give any to Kohath’s descendants because their service was with the holy objects, which they carried on their shoulders. 10 The leaders offered the dedication offering for the altar when it was anointed; they offered it in front of the altar.

item.title: Numbers 8
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 8

The Seven Lamps

         8:1The LORD told Moses 2 to have Aaron put up the 7 lamps to shine in front of the lampstand. 3 Aaron did that. 4 The lampstand was hammered gold from its base to its flowers in the pattern the LORD showed Moses.

item.title: Numbers 9
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 9

The Second Passover

1 In the first month, second year after the Israelites left Egypt, the LORD told Moses in the Wilderness of Sinai, 2-3 “Have Israel observe Passover as directed at its scheduled time: the fourteenth day of this month in the evening.” 4 Moses told the Israelites to observe it. 5 They did so in the Wilderness of Sinai the way the LORD commanded Moses.                                         Num 9:1-5

item.title: Numbers 10
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 10

The Silver Trumpets

         10:1The LORD told Moses,

            2 “Make two bugles out of hammered silver. Use them to gather the community or move the camp out. 3 Blow both bugles to gather the community at the Tent of Meeting. 4 Blow one bugle to gather just the leaders of thousands there.

item.title: Numbers 11
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 11

The People Complain

                            1 The people started complaining about their hardships. The LORD’s anger flared. It burned among them and burned up the outskirts of the camp. 2 The people cried out to Moses, and he prayed to the LORD, and the fire died down. 3 The name of that place became Taberah, because the LORD’s fire “burned” among them.

item.title: Numbers 12
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 12

Miriam and Aaron Oppose Moses

1 Miriam and Aaron criticized Moses for the Nubian woman he’d married. 2 “Has the LORD indeed only spoken through you? Hasn’t he spoken through us too?” The LORD heard it. ( 3 Moses was the most humble man in the world.) 4 At once, the LORD told Moses, Aaron, and Miriam, “Come out to the Tent of Meeting.” 5 He came down in a pillar of cloud and stood at the entrance to the Tent to call Aaron and Miriam. When they’d come forward, 6 he said,

item.title: Numbers 13
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 13

Spies Sent into Canaan

1 The LORD told Moses, 2 “Send one leader from each tribe to spy out Canaan that I’m giving to Israel.”

      3 Moses sent out these leaders from Paran:

                       4from Reuben,             Shammua son of Zaccur;

                        5 from Simeon,             Shaphat son of Hori;

item.title: Numbers 14
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 14

The People Rebel

            1 The community cried out loud that night 2 and complained to Moses and Aaron,

            “We should’ve died in Egypt! We should’ve died in the desert! 3 Why is the LORD taking us to this land to fall by the sword? Our wives and little ones will become spoils of war. Wouldn’t we be better off to go back to Egypt?”

item.title: Numbers 15
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 15

Laws About Sacrifices

1 The LORD told Moses to tell Israel,  

2 “When you go into the land I’m giving you, 3 make a burnt offering to me—a sacrifice to fulfill a special vow, a free-will offering, at your scheduled times. Make a pleasing aroma from the herd or flock.  

4 “When you present your offering, present a grain offering made from .11 bushel .1 ephah of fine flour mixed with 3 pints ¼ hin of olive oil.   5 Prepare wine for the liquid offering, 3 pints ¼ hin, with the burnt offering or sacrifice, for each lamb.

item.title: Numbers 16
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 16

Korah’s Rebellion

1 Korah son of Izhar son of Kohath son of Levi, along with Dathan and Abiram sons of Eliab and On son of Peleth (descendants of Reuben)— 2 rebelled together with 250 prominent, chosen leaders of the community. 3 They gathered against Moses and Aaron, and said,

“You’ve gone far enough. The whole community is holy, every one of them, and the LORD’s among them. Why place yourselves above the LORD’s assembly?”

item.title: Numbers 17
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 17

Aaron’s Rod That Budded

1 The LORD said to Moses, 

2 “Tell the Israelites to get a rod from each family leader. Write each man’s name on his rod 3 and write Aaron’s name on Levi’s rod. 4 Then put them in the Tent of Meeting in front of the Testimony where I meet you.   5 The rod of the man I choose will sprout. I’ll stifle the criticism of your critics.

item.title: Numbers 18
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 18

Duties of Priests and Levites

1 So the LORD told Aaron,

“You, your sons, and your father’s family will be responsible for the sanctuary. You and your sons will be responsible for the priesthood. 2 But take your relatives from the tribe of Levi to help you while you and your sons serve in the Tent of Testimony. 3 They’ll make preparations throughout the Tent, but they can’t approach the sanctuary furnishings or the altar, or they and you will die. 4 An outsider can’t come near you. 5 They’ll tend to preparations for the sanctuary and the altar so there won’t be any more anger on Israel. 6 I’ll give your fellow Levites to you, dedicated to the LORD, to service the Tent of Meeting. 7 But you and your sons will tend to everything around the altar and inside the curtain. I’m giving you the priesthood as a service; execute any outsider that comes near.”         Num 18:1-7

item.title: Numbers 19
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 19

Water of Purification

1 The LORD told Moses and Aaron,

2 “This is a statute of the LORD, ‘Tell the Israelites to bring you an unblemished red heifer that’s never worn a yoke. 3 Give it to Eleazar to take outside the camp and slaughter in his presence. 4 He’ll take some of its blood with his finger and sprinkle it toward the front of the Tent of Meeting seven times. 5 Then the heifer will be burned in front of him—hide, meat, blood, and refuse. 6 The priest will take cedar wood, hyssop, and scarlet material, and throw it onto the burning heifer. 7 Then he’ll wash his clothes, take a bath, and afterward come into the camp, but he’ll be ceremonially unclean till evening. 8 The one that burns it will also wash his clothes, take a bath, and be unclean till evening. 9 A man that’s clean will gather the heifer’s ashes and deposit them outside the camp in a clean place, and the community will keep it as water for removing impurity from sin. 10 The one that gathers the ashes will wash his clothes and be unclean till evening. A permanent statute for Israelites and foreigners living among them.

item.title: Numbers 20
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 20

The Death of Miriam

 1 The whole community came to the Wilderness of Zin in the first month and stayed at Kadesh. Miriam died and they buried her there.

Moses Strikes the Rock

2 The community didn’t have water and gathered against Moses and Aaron,

3 “If only we’d perished when our relatives perished in the LORD’s presence! 4 Why bring his assembly into this desert for us and our animals to die? 5 Why make us come up from Egypt to this wretched place? It’s not a place with grain, figs, grapes, or pomegranates; and there’s no water.”

item.title: Numbers 21
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 21

Arad Destroyed

1 When the Canaanite king of Arad, who lived in the Negev, heard that Israel was coming by way of Atharim, he fought Israel and took some captives. 2 Israel vowed to the LORD, “If you’ll deliver them to me, we’ll destroy their towns.” 3 He delivered them, and they “destroyed” them and their towns; the name of the place became Hormah.

item.title: Numbers 22
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 22

Balak Summons Balaam

            1 Israel traveled and camped in the plains of Moab across the Jordan from Jericho. 2 Balak son of Zippor saw what Israel did to the Amorites. 3 The people of Moab were afraid of them because there were so many of them. 4 The king of Moab told the elders in Midian, “This horde will eat up everything around us like an ox eats up grass in a pasture.” Balak, king of Moab at the time, 5 sent messengers to Balaam son of Beor at Pethor, near the River Euphrates in the land of his people. He called him and said,

item.title: Numbers 23
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 23

Balaam’s First Oracle

1 Then Balaam told Balak, “Build seven altars for me here and prepare seven bulls and rams.” 2 They offered a bull and a ram on each altar. 3 Balaam told him, “Stand beside your burnt offering, and I’ll go. Maybe the LORD will come to me, and I’ll tell you what he shows me.” He went to a bare hill. 4 God met him, and he said, “I’ve set up seven altars and offered a bull and a ram on each one.” 5 The LORD gave Balaam a message, “Go tell Balak such and such.” 6 He went back to Balak, who was standing by his burnt offering with the leaders of Moab.

item.title: Numbers 24
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 24

Balaam’s Third Oracle

24;1When Balaam saw that it pleased the LORD to bless Israel, he didn’t go like before to look for omens, but faced the open country. 2 He saw Israel camping by tribes, and God’s Spirit came on him. 3 He took up this oracle:

            “The oracle of Balaam son of Beor,

item.title: Numbers 25
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 25

Moab Seduces Israel

1 While Israel was camping in Shittim, the people started carrying on prostitution with the women of Moab. 2 They invited the people to the sacrifices to their gods, and the people ate and bowed to their gods. 3 In this way, Israel joined themselves to Baal of Peor, and the LORD was angry. 4 He told Moses, “Seize the ringleaders and execute them in broad daylight in my presence so my fierce anger will turn away from Israel.” 5 Moses told the judges, “Each of you put to death his men that have joined in worshiping Baal of Peor.”

item.title: Numbers 26
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 26

The Second Registration of Israel’s Troops

1 After the plague the LORD told Moses and Eleazar, 2 “Count by families the men 20 years old and up that can go to war.” 3 Moses and Eleazar told them in the plains of Moab by the Jordan across from Jericho, 4 “Count the men 20 years old and up.” The Israelites that came out of Egypt were as follows:

item.title: Numbers 27
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 27

The Daughters of Zelophehad

1 The daughters of Zelophehad son of (Hepher son of Gilead son of Machir son of Manasseh son of) Joseph came near: Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah. 2 They stood in front of Moses, Eleazar, and the leaders of the community at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting, and said,

item.title: Numbers 28
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 28

Daily Offerings

1-2 The LORD told Moses to tell Israel,

“Present my offerings by fire at their scheduled times, an aroma pleasing to me, my ‘food.’ 3 Make this offering by fire to the LORD every day: 2 male lambs a year old without defect. 4 Offer one in the morning, the other in the evening. 5 For a grain offering, offer .11 bushel .1 ephah of fine flour mixed with 3 pints ¼ hin of pressed olive oil. 6 It’s a daily burnt offering that was ordained at Mount Sinai. 7 The liquid offering that goes with it will be 3 pints ¼ hin of alcohol for each lamb, poured out in the Holy Place. 8 Offer the other lamb that evening the same way with its grain offering and liquid offering.

item.title: Numbers 29
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 29

Feast of Trumpets Offerings

1 “The first day, seventh month, hold a holy convocation; don’t work. Celebrate the Festival of Trumpets. 2 Offer a burnt offering as an aroma pleasing to the LORD: 1 bull, 1 ram, and 7 male lambs a year old without defect. 3 Offer with them their grain offerings of fine flour mixed with olive oil—.3 of a measure for the bull, .2 for the ram, 4 and .1 for each lamb. 5 Offer 1 male goat for a sin offering to atone for yourselves. 6 Offer them in addition to the burnt offering at the new moon and its grain offering, and the daily burnt offering and its grain offering. Offer their liquid offerings.

item.title: Numbers 30
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 30

Men’s Vows

                           1 Then Moses told the tribal leaders,  

            “This is what the LORD has commanded. 2 If a man makes a vow to the LORD or takes an oath that obligates him to do something, he must keep his word [Mt 5:33].

item.title: Numbers 31
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 31

Conquest of the Midianites

1 The LORD told Moses, 2 “Take revenge for Israel on the Midianites. Afterward you’ll pass on to your people.”

3 Moses told the people, “Arm men to attack Midian and carry out the LORD’s revenge on it. 4 Send 1,000 men from each tribe.”

item.title: Numbers 32
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 32

Reuben and Gad Settle in Gilead

1 Reuben and Gad had lots of livestock. When they saw that Jazer and Gilead had good grazing land, 2 they spoke to Moses, Eleazar the priest, and the leaders of the community,

 3 “Ataroth, Dibon, Jazer, Nimrah, Heshbon, Elealeh, Sebam, Nebo, and Beon— 4 land the LORD overthrew for Israel—is suitable for our livestock. 5 If we’re pleasing to you, give us this land instead of having us cross the Jordan.”

item.title: Numbers 33
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 33

Recounting Israel’s Journey

1 These are the segments of the journey Israel took when they left Egypt. 2 The LORD told Moses to write them down. 3 They traveled from Rameses on the fifteenth day, first month, the day after Passover. They left right in front of the Egyptians 4 while they were burying the firstborn that the LORD struck. The LORD also carried out judgments against their gods.

item.title: Numbers 34
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 34

Boundaries of the Land

         34:1-2The LORD told Moses to say to Israel,

“When you go into Canaan, 3 your south border will run from the Wilderness of Zin alongside Edom from the end of the Dead Sea eastward. 4 Your border will turn from the south to the ascent of Akrabbim and go on along to Zin. It will end south of Kadesh-barnea, go out to Hazaraddar, and on along to Azmon. 5 The border will turn from Azmon to where the River of Egypt empties into the Mediterranean Sea.

item.title: Numbers 35
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 35

Towns for the Levites

                  1 In the plains of Moab by the Jordan across from Jericho, the LORD said to Moses,

                  2“Tell Israel to give the Levites towns to live in, 3 along with pastureland for their cattle, herds, and animals. 4 The pastures will extend 1500ft out from the wall all around the city, 5 a 3,000ft diameter of pastureland. 6 Give the Levites 6 cities as refuge for a person to flee to that’s killed someone accidentally. 7 Give them 42 other towns with their pastures to make 48 total. 8 The towns you give them will be in proportion to the number of towns each tribe inherits.”

item.title: Numbers 36
item.description:
Numbers Chapter 36

Women Heirs of Property

1 The heads of clans came down from Gilead (son of Machir son of Manasseh son of Joseph) to Moses and Israel’s leaders. 2 They said,

“The LORD told you to allot land to Israel for inheritance, and to give the inheritance of Zelophehad our relative to his daughters. 3 But if they marry outside their tribe, their inheritance will move to the new tribe. 4 When Israel’s Year of Jubilee comes, it will become permanent in the new tribe and lost from ours.”
